darksteel_greatsword.name: Darksteel Greatsword
darksteel_greatsword.desc: Wrought from durable darksteel, these blades were popularised by the Ignition Union as tools against the Deep.

dawnshot.name: Dawnshot
dawnshot.desc: With an almost flawless silver sheen that prevents corrosion from saltwater, the Dawnshot was purpose-built by Rosen for his lengthy travels at sea. Its short range is compensated for by the almost wrist-snapping kickback of four barrels. Although, with the Summer Company Cannon making most naval investments in their territories obsolete, this weapon has fallen out of production

deepspindle.name: Deepspindle
deepspindle.desc: This rapier's blade was not forged in the traditional sense; rather, it was grown from the darkest scoria of the abyss, taking shape as a twisting, malevolent shadow. Tales speak of a noble warrior from Celtor who, after discovering ancient ministry passages, was consumed by a twisted curiosity and nurtured this vile blade. As the dark ritual progressed, the warrior's soul was pulled deeper into darkness, their essence decaying and their mind unraveling into madness. Ultimately succumbing to the depths, thus stirs the Deepspindle.

depthsplitter.name: Depthsplitter
depthsplitter.desc: An ancient Etrean twinblade, recently rediscovered as a fragment of driftland surfaced, revealing the remnants of a long-lost Etrean platoon that had perished and been claimed by the void ages ago. Now, this weapon, once thought lost to time, has re-emerged into circulation, carrying with it echoes of forgotten battles.

dissonant_chimecaller.name: Dissonant Chimecaller
dissonant_chimecaller.desc: An unexpectedly sophisticated staff with an antique design. In contrast to Knell's current state, it seems to originate from a time when he might have still been himself.

dormant_splinter.name: Dormant Splinter
dormant_splinter.desc: This Umbrite blade is unlike anything you've encountered. Marked and damaged by years, it remains both fluid and rigid in a way that defies understanding. When exposed to the other weapons in your collection, tiny fibers latch onto the surface, quivering before returning to their original state, as if the blade itself were tasting, searching for a worthy match.

dragoon.name: Dragoon
dragoon.desc: The mechanisms of this firearm are more of a meticulous make then their mass-produced cousins, and are often found as collector's items above the mantlepieces of the wealthy.

duskguard_axe.name: Duskguard Axe
duskguard_axe.desc: A mighty axe, held by the most esteemed of the Authority. Its weight and striking force deliver utterly brutal blows through the teeth of its foes.

duskshot.name: Duskshot
duskshot.desc: With an almost flawless silver sheen that prevents corrosion from saltwater, the Dawnshot was purpose-built by Rosen for his lengthy travels at sea. Its short range is compensated for by the almost wrist-snapping kickback of four barrels. Although, with the Summer Company Cannon making most naval investments in their territories obsolete, this weapon has fallen out of production.

edenbrand_hellcoil.name: Edenbrand Hellcoil
edenbrand_hellcoil.desc: Imbued with the breath of a true Edenkite, this cannon holds the fading remnants of one slain by a Northern Tiran hunting party of considerable renown. Trapping the beast in a ravine, the band brought it low with an onslaught of cannonfire from the cliffs above. However, as fate would have it, their return journey was cut short by a storm that had their vessel veer off into the Voidsea, taking them and their prize off the map. Now, its bitterly cold soulfire has found its way into your hands.

enforcers_axe.name: Enforcer's Axe
enforcers_axe.desc: The titans known as Enforcers are faithful servants of the Cathedral, rarely found outside its grounds. It is said that they are the tortured souls of those who have failed the Trial, twisted into fiends that deny mortal souls salvation.

enforcers_blade.name: Enforcer's Blade
enforcers_blade.desc: The titans known as Enforcers are faithful servants of the Cathedral, rarely found outside its grounds. It is said that they are the tortured souls of those who have failed the Trial, twisted into fiends that deny mortal souls salvation.

enforcers_hammer.name: Enforcer's Hammer
enforcers_hammer.desc: The titans known as Enforcers are faithful servants of the Cathedral, rarely found outside its grounds. It is said that they are the tortured souls of those who have failed the Trial, twisted into fiends that deny mortal souls salvation.

ethiron_curseshield.name: Ethiron Curseshield
ethiron_curseshield.desc: A shield forged to brave the harshest of Ethironal winds, handcrafted by those who sought fortune amidst ruin.

eye_of_malice.name: Eye of Malice
eye_of_malice.desc: Fashioned from the remnants of Caeranthilâ€™s Doom, the blade undulates like a living thing, lurching and clawing at your foes from afar. The Submergence of the Southern Luminant was not solely perpetrated by the Horror that plunged Celtor into the Depths below, but by countless other smaller tragedies drowned out by the vastness of calamity. Of these, the leviathan known as the Doom of Caeranthil, is named for the land it too dragged below. In many respects, knowledge of Caeranthil only persists within the lens of nightmares that consumed it. The culture, the food, the laughter, the in-jokes, the petty disputes, the fashions they styled themselves afterâ€¦ How can we experience these things if thereâ€™s no one left to remember them?

eyes_of_ethiron.name: Eyes of Ethiron
eyes_of_ethiron.desc: TheÂ forge's touch peels back history's layered retellings and scrapes bare the blade's past.Â Godstorm, the wind that bore a shadow. An eye at the heart of each furling spiral. Long beforeÂ DrallisÂ tamed it, and beforeÂ the TidesÂ drew in, it was in contemplation of the devastation it wrought that Mankind cultivated the art that would becomeÂ Galebreathing. Yet, owing perhaps to its muse, this nascent art would also become the foundation ofÂ Shadowcasting. In some senses, it was only in the years where theÂ GodstormÂ was dormant that the wind and shadow could be considered separate.

falchion.name: Falchion
falchion.desc: A single-edged sabre often wielded by conscripted Authority militiamen due to its relative ease of production.

fang_and_coil.name: Fang and Coil
fang_and_coil.desc: The serpentine martial arts ofÃ‚Â Etrea.

first_light.name: First Light
first_light.desc: Blade of Venn Kaelhand, a disciple of Pleeksty, who immersed themself much too deeply in the study of beasts altered by Korilfiend's influence. Met their fate in the jaws of a unique specimen of Megalodaunt said to have been exposed to the vents of Korilfiend.

flareblood_kamas.name: Flareblood Kamas
flareblood_kamas.desc: Weapon of a former student of the 4th Prophet who quickly moved up through the ranks of the Ministry due to their own ingenuity and modifications upon Zi'eer's puppet strings. Met their demise at the hands of the Maestro, posing as a Vigil Instructor in order to gather intel. Seeing through the disguise, the Maestro challenged the applicant instructor and forced them to reveal their hand. With no more tricks left up their sleeve, they fell before the Maestro's arsenal, their own handiwork joining the collection.

flintlock.name: Flintlock
flintlock.desc: The son of a village blacksmith, a young Gunsmith Rosen took to the seas and returned an older man, bringing with him the spark of inspiration that brought firearms into the world. The modern flintlock is an improved and lightweight version of Gunsmith's prototype now found in every Luminant.

fondant_splitter.name: Fondant Splitter
fondant_splitter.desc: Is this safe to eat? We'll see...

forge_greathammer.name: Forge Greathammer
forge_greathammer.desc: It is said that blacksmiths always keep one of these handy should someone ever stiff them on the bill.

forgotten_gladius.name: Forgotten Gladius
forgotten_gladius.desc: Found at the hip of horseback officers during the Canticlysm. For many of the post-tidal years it was but a curio for petty nobles to fawn over. Despite its moniker, it was made fashionable by young naval officers of the Authority who saw value in the trinkets they'd inherited, dragging the blade out from obscurity.

formless_shard.name: Formless Shard
formless_shard.desc: Once a thing. Now just a shrapnel.

fractine.name: Fractine
fractine.desc:
Ill-fated blade of void, once whole but nevermore.
Bound through Vow to the soul of a former wielder; soul now claimed by the void, shattered, fragments; through Vow, blade now fractured remnant; semblance of that which once claimed it.

frost_gauntlets.name: Frost Gauntlets
frost_gauntlets.desc: Perfect for a Knuckle Sandwich, served cold.

frostthorn.name: Frostthorn
frostthorn.desc: The elaborate spring-powered mechanism contained within this weapon allows its layered blades to fan out in multitude. The metal emanates a constant chill, ringing lime crystals upon contact with any surface. Doubtless, an unusual weapon for any Kyrsan to wield, given where you found it. Perhaps it is a trophy of war, retooled anew for its purpose?...

gale_pale.name: Gale Pale
gale_pale.desc: An artisanal reuse of an old faithful greathammer. Coated in silver, this Pale Morning can glide through the winds current like nothing to maul it's opponents.

gaunts_of_enmity.name: Gaunts of Enmity
gaunts_of_enmity.desc: Gauntlets of great strength, forged long ago to fortify the wielder and shatter the souls of the weak.

gilded_knife.name: Gilded Knife
gilded_knife.desc: A knife of fine craftsmanship, bearing an equally fine sharpness.

golden_swordfish.name: Golden Swordfish
golden_swordfish.desc: The fabled catch of an angler without parallel. The sea trembles in your wake.

gran_sudaruska.name: Gran Sudaruska
gran_sudaruska.desc: In her dying breaths, Sudaruska the Unbroken infused her very being into this weapon, cutting through hundreds of ancient Ministry as the last front to keep Minityrsa safe. Now that her mortal shell is no more, she has become Gran Sudaruska.

gremorian_longspear.name: Gremorian Longspear
gremorian_longspear.desc: Beyond the safety of walls, these long spears are used by the Children of Navae to defend themselves out in the wilderness.

guiding_star.name: Guiding Star
guiding_star.reqs: guiding_star := 75r MED, 25r INT OR 25r WLL OR 25r CHA
guiding_star.desc: A bow of sacred reverence for the Children of Navae, the Guiding Star is wielded during the Festival of Lights by aspirants who seek to prove themselves in its trials. During these trials, competitors must hunt a wolf blindfolded, using only their other senses to track and remain hidden. Should the wolf sink its fangs into the competitor, or leave any mark upon them, they are disqualified. The bow itself is also somewhat of a curiosity, as its arrows are conjured from the Ether of its wielder and guided by their own will. Many of the previous winners of the trials speak of the Guiding Star speaking to them in a soothing voice, pushing them in the right direction.

hailbreaker.name: Hailbreaker
hailbreaker.desc: With the development of the Railblade, the Union had gained an edge over the Divers in their disputes over delving sites. In defiance of this, the mechanists of the Citadel changed their approach to equipment production and worked closely with Authority Weaponsmiths to design a cold-hearted blade capable of piercing through monster hide and freezing their innards solid. The end result was a weapon that not only excelled in missions, but also a in skirmishes against the more territorial squads of Union expeditioners. Sometimes spite works as the greatest motivator of change.

halberd.name: Halberd
halberd.desc: A standard issue halberd of the Authority, often seen in larger skirmishes.

hallowscleave.name: Hallowscleave
hallowscleave.desc: A blade said to be fashioned by the Spellhardt family to settle disputes in a festive style. It's bound to catch an odd gaze at a standard tavern, but one must simply express their Hallowtide spirit.

heros_blade_of_flame.name: Hero's Blade of Flame
heros_blade_of_flame.desc: A blade said to be enruned with a passage of the Song, it evokes the overwhelming heat of geothermal vents

heros_blade_of_frost.name: Hero's Blade of Frost
heros_blade_of_frost.desc: A blade said to be enruned with a passage of the song, it evokes the freezing oblivion of the cold wastes.

heros_blade_of_lightning.name: Hero's Blade of Lightning
heros_blade_of_lightning.desc: A blade said to be enruned with a passage of the song, it evokes the hair-raising trepidation for the incoming storm.

heros_blade_of_shadow.name: Hero's Blade of Shadow
heros_blade_of_shadow.desc: A blade said to be enruned with a passage of the song, it evokes the merciless void of the darkest shadows.

heros_blade_of_wind.name: Hero's Blade of Wind
heros_blade_of_wind.desc: A blade said to be enruned with a passage of the song, it evokes the inescapable force of a hurricane.

hunting_bow.name: Hunting Bow
hunting_bow.desc: RabbitÂ orÂ duck, it doesn't really matter the season.

icarus_sun_shield.name: Icarus Sun Shield
icarus_sun_shield.desc: The shields ofÃ‚Â LegionÃ‚Â shieldbearers are often decorated with the visages of famous warriors they admire.

icepick.name: Icepick
icepick.desc: It's almost as if it grew straight out of the ice, but that couldn't be possible, could it?

imperators_edge.name: Imperator's Edge
imperators_edge.desc: A gargantuan greatspear, its tip infused with the very essence of light, shines like a beacon of unyielding strength and fortitude. Carried at the forefront of the Hundred Legions, it illuminates their path with a blinding brilliance, a symbol of resilience that pierces the shadows and leads them to inevitable victory.

imperial_staff.name: Imperial Staff
imperial_staff.desc: Legendary staff of a Legion Praetorian of ages past. In his hands it's said to have wiped whole towns off the face of the map with one swing, and to have rapidly expanded the outer reaches of their territory. The veracity of these claims is of some dispute, but the peculiar properties of the weapon do lend some credence to its reputation.

imperium_kata.name: Imperium Kata
imperium_kata.desc: The legendary martial arts style of theÃ‚Â Legion. Emphasises the sweeping motions of wind.

inquisitors_thorn.name: Inquisitor's Thorn
inquisitors_thorn.desc: Deceivingly swift, this blade was once used to pierce through the carapaces of the indomitable Crustaceous Rex by members of the ill-fated Authority 'Expeditionary Corps'.

iron_birch.name: Iron Birch
iron_birch.desc: The smiths of the Hive will often decorate their weaponry with the unique flora of their homes. Some of the world's best existing botanical records are found on the heads of axes.

iron_blunderbuss.name: Iron Blunderbuss
iron_blunderbuss.desc: Rustic and shabby in design, this application of Rosen's gunsmith knowledge takes a more aggressive, risky approach in usage. While it's range is lacking, it shows nothing but brutality in close combat encounters as one of Summer Company's earliest gunsmithing experiments.

iron_requiem.name: Iron Requiem
iron_requiem.desc: Reverse-engineered from stolen Summer Company weapons shipments, in recent times Ironsingers have found a lucrative profession in the creation of weaponry and equipment. Being the birthplace of the art's revival, the Ignition Union have funneled this wealth into the production of weapons imbued with active Ironsing components.

iron_spear.name: Iron Spear
iron_spear.desc: A spear perfect for poking at a safe distance

iron_twinblade.name: Iron Twinblade
iron_twinblade.desc: A modest twinblade, inspired by the whirling Etrean blades of ancient times. Though some dismiss it as unwieldy and impractical, those who choose to wield this weapon seek something far greater than mere convenience. Their focus is not on ease of use, but on the artistry, power, and relentless momentum that only a blade like this can unleash.

ironcry.name: Ironcry
ironcry.desc: An old, antique hunting bow with a powerful draw, its best days seem behind it.

irontusk.name: Irontusk
irontusk.desc: A spear passed down by your ancestors.

jus_karita.name: Jus Karita
jus_karita.desc: The traditional martial arts style of the Justicars.

kanabo.name: Kanabo
kanabo.desc: Once a staple weapon in Etrea's Arsenal, renowned for its scale and brute force, it was wielded by the heavy infantry of the King's Guard. These ironclad warriors used kanabos to enforce the King's will and maintain order across the Etrean Luminant. Today, its use has become significantly less common, with Etrea favouring the development of its blades instead.

karita_plating.name: Karita Plating
karita_plating.desc: Developed by Konga the 2nd, these set of shin platings are suited to aid the user in sliding along tough surfaces much more easily. This strengthens their ability to be able to attack using the infamous Slider Style of the Konga lineage.

katana.name: Katana
katana.desc: An exquisite blade of Etrean make, it's said to have put the early Kingdom of Etrea's name on the map.

khan_shield.name: Khan Shield
khan_shield.desc: The shields of Legion shieldbearers are often decorated with the visages of famous warriors they admire.

kindred_edict.name: Kindred Edict
kindred_edict.reqs: kindred_edict := 50r MED, 30r STR OR 30r FTD OR 30r AGL
kindred_edict.desc: A blade forged from pale metal recovered from Celestial crash-sites by Starkindred artisans. Nearly weightless, this type of blade is a staple in the West amongst adherents of the faith. Once one has spilt enough of their own blood in service of the Church, they are bestowed a weapon thrice-bathed in the sacred ichor of a Celestial.

kite_shield.name: Kite Shield
kite_shield.desc: A fine shield to hold back the horrors of the world.

krulian_knife.name: Krulian Knife
krulian_knife.desc: An exceptionally sharp knife forged by unknown means. Carries an aura of intense foreboding.

kyrsglaive.name: Kyrsglaive
kyrsglaive.desc: An ancient spear of kyrsan craftsmanship, its presence beyond the second layer is a miracle. few have ever been found by humanity. unlike their usual weapons, this spear was forged to control the raw, untamed power of their kin. made from the remains of the primal, cartilage wielding creatures they once shared the world with, this arsenal was an attempt to harness the strength of their wild precursors that had been forsaken in favor of the cold precision of their spears of frost.

kyrsieger.name: Kyrsieger
kyrsieger.desc: The fall of Constellia was first heralded by a devastating piercing through its crystalline arches. Perhaps the most renowned member of the Kyrsan arsenal, the blade's explosive power can only be invoked through a solemn prayer to Ethiron. The words of this prayer worm their way into the minds of its bearer willing or not.

kyrstear.name: Kyrstear
kyrstear.desc: Suited to the unpredictable movements of the Kyrsgarde.

kyrswynter.name: Kyrswynter
kyrswynter.desc: Forged in an age lost to history and salvaged from the Eternal Gale, this relic hails from a forgotten era. As the last whispered remnant of its kind, it comes from a time before the people of New Kyrsa fell under the command of the Drowned God Ethiron. The spear is extremely cold to the touch, with an unsettling chill that clings to your skin, leaving a lingering numbness. It embodies the delicate and refined artistry of the Kyrsgarde, showcasing their elegance and sophistication prior to their corruption. Though the Kyrsgarde still wield spears of frost, the craftsmanship of their modern weaponry pales in comparison, having become crude and uninspired under Ethiron's thrall.

legion_cestus.name: Legion Cestus
legion_cestus.desc: Cestus used by members of the Hundred Legions.

legion_kata.name: Legion Kata
legion_kata.desc: The legendary martial arts style of theÃ‚Â Legion. Emphasises the sweeping motions of wind.

lights_final_toll.name: Light's Final Toll
lights_final_toll.desc: A reminder that all songs must come to an end.

lumensplitter.name: Lumensplitter
lumensplitter.desc: A vile, jagged blade fashioned in mimicry of the jaws that claimed the isle of Caeranthil, Celtor's resplendent gate. Much like the frenzy that follows when a body hits the water, the South's submergence was a cascade of calamities one after another.

mace.name: Mace
mace.desc: A simple blunt weapon effective at breaking guards.

markors_inheritor.name: Markor's Inheritor
markors_inheritor.desc: The Blade Scholars of Markor, said to catalog the world itself, travel from land to land collecting information by force of their blades. Mastering the styles of combat from various stretches of the sea, they practice their art through the blade itself.

messer.name: Messer
messer.desc: Considered by many to be an overgrown kitchen knife, the Messer is a favourite of rangers and beast hunters the world over.

metal_greatsword.name: Metal Greatsword
metal_greatsword.desc: A sword you summoned using your opponent's metal rods.

moon_blades.name: Moon Blades
moon_blades.desc: A twisted weapon devised by the Scholar of the Burning Blood, Chaser, during his apprenticeship under the Second Prophet. A seething concoction of tainted blood swirls within, waiting to be unburdened of its prison.

moonseye_scalpel.name: Moonseye Scalpel
moonseye_scalpel.desc: Congratulations! You've won the Rifle Spear lottery.

moppet.name: Moppet
moppet.desc: Just as we see likeness in objects, Vows can often be empowered by the imitative correspondence between an effigy and its target.

needles_eye.name: Needle's Eye
needles_eye.desc: Not much is known about these rapiers other than the fact that they're a reproduction of Duchess Thera Erisia's rapier, which she is said to have died with in a dispute while being vastly outnumbered, the tale goes that she actually made it through the onslaught but died from blood loss in the arms of her noble son Nir, who took her mantle shortly after.

night_axe.name: Night Axe
night_axe.desc: The Night Axe, a weapon of little contemporary renown, was forged by the ancient folk of Minityrsa and wielded by Ekrim of the Rouhlx Dyad, who came to be known as the Third's Eclipse. Paired with its luminous counterpart, Daybreak, the Night Axe cast long shadows of fear across the old settlements. Its history is whispered in tales of unstoppable might and the haunting interplay of night and day. Shaky accounts speak of the pair meeting a grim fate at the hands of a power they once worshipped, sealing their legend in shade.

nocturne.name: Nocturne
nocturne.desc: These twin blades of Celestial make carry an eerie semblance of consciousness. Along their lengths run glowing tapestries of ethereal moonlight. If one listens closely, you can almost make out whispers, or perhaps cries. The blades are known to grow heavier and more unwieldy when used for unjust purposes, as though they judge the actions of its user. As such, it is thought that only a person of unwavering integrity can hope to wield them to their truest potential.

officer_saber.name: Officer Saber
officer_saber.desc: The characteristic saber of an Authority Officer. Typically used in Authority raids on disputed territories.

old_world_greatshield.name: Old World Greatshield
old_world_greatshield.desc: A shield designed intricately, most likely from a pre-Canticlysm era. Its intricate design suggests previous use in royalty, although it cannot be said for sure.

pale_briar.name: Pale Briar
pale_briar.desc: Staff of a Cardinal of the Starkindred Church, said to be coated in Celestial Ichor to grant it the investment of the Stars. This particular iteration fell into the hands of the Maestro during his training with the Legions in an excursion against the Starkindred Church.

pale_morning.name: Pale Morning
pale_morning.desc: A hammer of exceptionally fine make, a replica of a famous hammer wielded by a Canor warlord during the First Century of the New World.

parrying_dagger.name: Parrying Dagger
parrying_dagger.desc: A dagger that is used more as a pivot for parrying than as a weapon of its own right.

petras_anchor.name: Petra's Anchor
petras_anchor.desc: The anchor of the vessel "Petra's Dawn", famed for its voyage between Luminants without the aid of a Lightkeeper's Gift. The ship, of course, did not make the return journey.

phantomcleave.name: Phantomcleave
phantomcleave.desc: A simple katana of fine glass, once carried by an esteemed vigil swordsman. Accounts from their crew claim that after an accidental detour into the Voidsea, both swordsman and blade were caught in a strange vortex. Whether the story is true remains unclear. Only the weapon, embedded in the cracked hull, and a handful of dehydrated survivors were recovered, later found washed ashore at the feet of Etris, with no sign of its former master.

pleekstys_inferno.name: Pleeksty's Inferno
pleekstys_inferno.desc: In the Tiran homelands, weapons are often fashioned using knapped volcanic glass like obsidian. Though brittle, the prismatic blades embedded in the sides are sharper than any blade of metalwork. Following the ascent of Amorus Pleeksty and his Pioneering of Flamecharm, many Tirans worked floral patterns into their Macuahuitls in reverence of the Deepwoken. Of these Macuahuitls, this particular specimen was a gift to Pleeksty himself, imbued with the eruptive and scorching heat of magma, and fit only to be wielded by those who truly walk in his footsteps.

prototype_railblade.name: Prototype Railblade
prototype_railblade.desc: A prototype weapon devised by the Ignition Union in the hopes of improving their odds against the denizens of the Deep.

purple_cloud.name: Purple Cloud
purple_cloud.desc: A mesmerizing blade of obsidian and violet, you feel as if the sword has a soul of its own. It seems wise to only unsheathe it momentarily.

quartztone_pickaxe.name: Quartztone Pickaxe
quartztone_pickaxe.desc: A pickaxe fashioned from an exceptionally large node of quartz. The crystal has been painstakingly tuned to the frequency of a Mantra, conjuring its effects on use.

quickfang.name: Quickfang
quickfang.desc: A rapier passed down by your ancestors.

railblade.name: Railblade
railblade.desc: A now legendary blade devised by the Ignition Union to exterminate the terrifying foes lurking deeper within the Depths. Said to constantly generate a warding heat that protects the wearer against the insidious chill of the Eternal Gale.

razor_cutlass.name: Razor Cutlass
razor_cutlass.desc: A serrated cutlass fashioned after the famously brutal bite of a Lionfish.

red_death.name: Red Death
red_death.reqs: red_death := 30r MED, 25r INT OR 25r WLL OR 25r CHA, 45r SDW, 35r BLD
red_death.desc: Tool of the Ministry Heartbleeders, a covert cell under the 2nd Prophet's authority who gather information through ruthless interrogation tactics. Imbued with a hybrid meter of two separate verses of the Song, it utilises Bloodrend to remove all organic traces, and Shadowcast to remove all traces of ether. Even the metal itself is oleophobic, rejecting organic oils and fluids, so the spear itself carries no prints or traces.

repeater.name: Repeater
repeater.desc: A recent invention of the Summer Company's gunsmiths, a firearm with a high rate of fire.

rifle_spear.name: Rifle Spear
rifle_spear.desc: A modification upon Gunsmith Rosen's designs produced by Gunsmith Hierka, the former protÃƒÆ’Ã‚Â©gÃƒÆ’Ã‚Â© of Rosen. It's said that Hierka hid his origins as a member of the Authority's Bureau of Research in order to steal the Summer Company's secrets.

rimebreakers.name: Rimebreakers
rimebreakers.desc: Immersed within the Ethironal storm and infused with Kyrsan frost, these knucklebone fistwraps are a poignant reflection of the current state of the Kyrsgarde. Once a proud and mighty people, the Kyrsans now find themselves buffeted, suffocating beneath an oppressive force - with little hope for salvation. Perhaps a similar fate awaits your foes, should they face these implements in combat.

rite_of_authority.name: Rite of Authority
rite_of_authority.desc: An incredibly well crafted saber, if you could even call it that. It's superb at channeling thundercall through it, arcs jumping between the rivets that coat its length.

ritual_sacrifice.name: Ritual Sacrifice
ritual_sacrifice.desc: Is sacrifice not the most potent of all rituals?

ritual_spear.name: Ritual Spear
ritual_spear.desc: A spear that was once utilised in Gremor rituals pledged to Navae, but has now been appropriated across the Eastern Luminant

rockmaller.name: Rockmaller
rockmaller.desc: It's just a small, quarried chunk of a far more powerful stone. Silent, unremarkable, about as lively as... well, a rock on a stick. But as soon as you grip it, you feel that raw, untamed power humming beneath its rough surface. This isn't about wielding a polished weapon, it's about the sheer, brutal devastation this humble fragment is ready to unleash in your hands.

rosens_hellflame.name: Rosen's Hellflame
rosens_hellflame.desc: The sources of inspiration and insight utilized by Rosen on his journeys becomes clearer in some of his one-off pieces. Capable of unleashing a torrent of scorching flames at will, the Hellflame is no exception. There was a time when Rosen never left his workshop, fevered as he was by a fell drive to draw out the knowledge he had gained in its entirety, like a sinner in a confessional.

rosens_peacemaker.name: Rosen's Peacemaker
rosens_peacemaker.desc: A mastercraft forged by Gunsmith Rosen himself, a revolutionary piece of weaponry that changed the art of combat forever. They carry high value on the market, due to both the historical importance of this firearm, as well as it's effectiveness.

rosens_roscoe.name: Rosen's Roscoe
rosens_roscoe.desc: A sleek hand cannon, wrought from meticulous ironsmithing. The Roscoe marks Lumen's First generation of true cylindrical barrel revolvers. Though slower than the Company's repeater models, it delivers unmatched power and precision, earning Rosen's student a well deserved payday for their innovation

sanguine_transfuser.name: Sanguine Transfuser
sanguine_transfuser.desc: You watch the once solid splinter animate within the cold forge; the fragmented blade freely imbibing its now biotic form. The corroded dagger falls away, leaving the splinter and blood to dance through the helical mold. As they reach the now-fused Hemobloom, the splinter diverges, rejected by the crystallizing blood now solidly cast into its progenitor's form. Though the relic was destroyed and your experiment failed, what remains may be worth what was lost.

scalesplitter.name: Scalesplitter
scalesplitter.desc: An ancient Etrean twinblade, recently rediscovered as a fragment of driftland surfaced, revealing the remnants of a long-lost Etrean platoon that had perished and been claimed by the void ages ago. Now, this weapon, once thought lost to time, has re-emerged into circulation, carrying with it echoes of forgotten battles.

scimitar.name: Scimitar
scimitar.desc: A curved blade that hails from the savannah of Markor. Though now assimilated into the Central Authority, the people of Markor still celebrate a rich culture.

scoundrels_saber.name: Scoundrel's Saber
scoundrels_saber.desc: A sturdy Celtorian saber with a weighted pommel, once wielded by Traan Zakshun. Known for feints and dirty tricks, he struck foes with the heavy pommel as often as the blade.

serpents_edge.name: Serpent's Edge
serpents_edge.desc: Despite popular opinion, the Serpent's Edge is actually a blade of Capra make, as opposed to one of Etrean make. It cuts just as well as any Etrean blade, however.

shadebolt.name: Shadebolt
shadebolt.desc: Bow of an ancient deep dwelling Ganymede nomad. Through taboo rites, Umbrite was bent to their will, shaped into tools that seemed almost alive. Some named the figure Shaman. Most never learned a name.

shattered_katana.name: Shattered Katana
shattered_katana.desc: Fashioned after the legendary blade said to be wielded by the King of Etrea, the Splinterblade. An exceptionally lethal blade that makes short work of heavy armor.

shotel.name: Shotel
shotel.desc: A blade from Markor prized by warriors for its ability to effortlessly slip past the guards of foes.

silver_dagger.name: Silver Dagger
silver_dagger.desc: A gleaming dagger with a dusting of silver. Beloved by outlaws who fancy themselves fashionable.

silversix.name: Silversix
silversix.desc: A pistol passed down by your ancestors.

skullpiercer.name: Skullpiercer
skullpiercer.desc: Upon holding the blade, it feels as if the skulls adorning it are warm, somehow. What is this, truly?

skyreap_blade.name: Skyreap Blade
skyreap_blade.desc: The ribs of this blade seem to possess an otherworldly durability, unheard of within these lands. Some rumor that they may be bones of the Edenkite themselves.

smouldering_hallow.name: Smouldering Hallow
smouldering_hallow.desc: Imbued with twisted roots of Ardfall, this cannon is unlike any other. Forged by the smiths of the Floating Keep, its festive gourds unleash devastating cheer. You can't help but grin a little inside when you fire it.

soulshot.name: Soulshot
soulshot.desc: How heavy is this thing when fully loaded?

soulthorn.name: Soulthorn
soulthorn.desc: Swirling within, the core of this blade is said to have been siphoned from the Moonseye by a Celestial traveler on their voyage across the skies. Its edge transmits a cutting anguish upon its foe, carving spiritual wounds. Its previous wielder, once known as the Moonlight Prince, is said to have been driven to madness by the voices trapped within. Holding it, you can hear it whispering speaking directly into your mind. The voices within lament their twisted fate; stolen away from their heavenly vault to ultimately meet the same fate as the Drowned.

soulwrought_axe.name: Soulwrought Axe
soulwrought_axe.desc: A weapon brought out from your soul.

soulwrought_dagger.name: Soulwrought Dagger
soulwrought_dagger.desc: A weapon brought out from your soul.

soulwrought_greatsword.name: Soulwrought Greatsword
soulwrought_greatsword.desc: A weapon brought out from your soul.

soulwrought_gun.name: Soulwrought Gun
soulwrought_gun.desc: A weapon brought out from your soul.

soulwrought_longsword.name: Soulwrought Longsword
soulwrought_longsword.desc: A weapon brought out from your soul.

soulwrought_spear.name: Soulwrought Spear
soulwrought_spear.desc: A weapon brought out from your soul.

sovereign_bangle.name: Sovereign Bangle
sovereign_bangle.desc: An ancient bangle bestowed upon the disciples of Theros, its bearer once honored among the Legion.

sovereigns_punishment.name: Sovereign's Punishment
sovereigns_punishment.desc: A ceremonial hammer meant to represent the strength of the Authority Law. Considered unwieldy and unusable for battle. Until you picked it up.

steel_maul.name: Steel Maul
steel_maul.desc: A sturdy maul perfect for knocking walls and people down alike.

stiletto.name: Stiletto
stiletto.desc: A remarkably thin blade often likened to (and used as) a toothpick.

stoneheart.name: Stoneheart
stoneheart.desc: A hammer pieced together from the remains of one of Duke Ishamon Erisia's Golems. Conjured by the Duke's Resonance, they once patrolled his estate in Celtor before its fall. From the reports of spies hired by rival nobles in the city, he was only able to support a limited number of golems - his acquisition of the Forge of Sin seems to have extended his capacity by other, more sinister means.

stormseye.name: Stormseye
stormseye.desc: Pilfered from Union shipments, the Summer Company saw the tremendous energy stored within Kyrsan Medallions and fashioned this uniquely violent prototype. Rosen allegedly burst into raucous laughter when presented with the draft, immediately giving his approval.

summer_hullwreaker.name: Summer Hullwreaker
summer_hullwreaker.desc: An antique creation from the Summer Company, built to place battleship-level firepower in the palms of your hands. Once hailed a masterpiece, it was soon overshadowed by the lighter rifles, yet in the hands of someone strong enough to wield it, it can still unleash devastation unmatched by newer models.

summer_rifle.name: Summer Rifle
summer_rifle.desc: A standard issue firearm produced by the Summer Company. A reliable firearm, though somewhat less elegant than their more expensive models.

sword.name: Sword
sword.desc: A blade of fine craft, likely an heirloom of whoever held the blade before it was pilfered from their remains.

tanto.name: Tanto
tanto.desc: A dagger forged by a Etrean smith, the Tanto is said to bear the same durability as the Katana, but in a smaller profile

targe.name: Targe
targe.desc: A round, wooden shield often accompanied by spear or short blade.

the_barrel.name: The Barrel
the_barrel.desc: Once mounted on a royal Etrean galleon, this bulky cannon was salvaged from a wreck by the Summer Company and adapted into a handheld design; still cumbersome, according to those who used it before its eventual retirement.

the_death_knell.name: The Death Knell
the_death_knell.desc: A reminder that all songs must come to an end.

the_flippers_of_fate.name: The Flippers of Fate
the_flippers_of_fate.reqs: the_flippers_of_fate := 70r LHT, 30r INT OR 30r WLL OR 30r CHA

the_golden_fool.name: The Golden Fool
the_golden_fool.desc: Warriors bold with straps of gold, that dazzle like the sun!

the_long_tong_of_the_law.name: The Long Tong of The Law
the_long_tong_of_the_law.reqs: the_long_tong_of_the_law := 70r HVY, 30r INT OR 30r WLL OR 30r CHA

the_pastry_paster.name: The Pastry Paster
the_pastry_paster.reqs: the_pastry_paster := 70r MED, 30r INT OR 30r WLL OR 30r CHA
the_pastry_paster.desc: Anyone can cook, but only the fearless can be great.

the_paths_defender.name: The Path's Defender
the_paths_defender.desc: A venerated shield, donned only by those who have walked their own Path as they see fit.

the_weaving_web.name: The Weaving Web
the_weaving_web.desc: Aww, look at the little guy though!...

trident_spear.name: Trident Spear
trident_spear.desc: A surprisingly effective weapon despite its previous use by fishermen.

umbrite_witherblade.name: Umbrite Witherblade
umbrite_witherblade.desc: Once a derelict frame, now restored by your offerings, pulsing quicker and quicker... Its fibers twist and crack, reaching out to consume the cutlass, its shape warping into a rough mirage of the weapon. Infused with minerals it once knew, channeling the warped umbrite through its slick inky veins. Each strike drains the very soul of your foes, leaving them withered and undone.

unsung_scythern.name: Unsung Scythern
unsung_scythern.desc: Forged in the tumultuous fires of the Canticlysm, the Unsung Scythern stands as a testament to the resilience of humanity in the face of chaos. Crafted by ancient hands, its blade bears the weight of forgotten histories and lost civilizations. This weapon channels the echoes of a bygone era, whispering tales of survival. With each swing, it carves through the veil of time.

vapormaw_carbine.name: Vapormaw Carbine
vapormaw_carbine.desc: An experimental steam-powered firearm whose pressured steam reservoir delivers exceptional kinetic force, making it capable of remarkably destructive output.

vigil_longsword.name: Vigil Longsword
vigil_longsword.desc: Despite the popularity of the Katana, the Vigil Longsword represents Etrea's need for more cost-efficient production processes in blades. Regarded as an equally fine blade, nonetheless.

vortex_echo.name: Vortex Echo
vortex_echo.desc: A small, pulsating vortex of Ethironal gale churns endlessly within this timepiece, driving its resonant ticks in eternal rhythm. Its ornate ivory white frame radiates an unnatural chill, as though the air surrounding it twists under the weight of some otherworldly force. Reaching in to grasp the ethereal chain, a sudden rush sources through you, a sensation like no other, being wrenched through the fabric of time itself. In those fleeting moments, you feel yourself grow just a bit older.

warden_ceremonial_sword.name: Warden Ceremonial Sword
warden_ceremonial_sword.desc: The ceremonial blade of an Authority Warden. Despite their position in ceremony and stately affairs, the Wardens of the Authority are some of the fiercest warriors in the Authority's arsenal. Their presence in public affairs is seen as a move to strike fear into their enemies.

warmonger.name: Warmonger
warmonger.desc: Warmonger hate. Warmonger destroy. Warmonger kill.

way_of_navae.name: Way of Navae
way_of_navae.desc: The mysterious martial arts style of theÃ‚Â Children of Navae. Each strike is said to be dedicated to a different constellation.

weal_and_woe.name: Weal and Woe
weal_and_woe.desc: Twin spears hewn from a single massive shard of umbral obsidian by twin Shadowcasters. Twins are thought to have a unique compatibility with Vows, their souls' murmurs being compliments of one another. Through identical Vows taken by twins, a form of entanglement and pairing can be granted to objects. The wielder of one of these objects can always sense the whereabouts of its counterpart. These spears are no exception with their entanglement allowing its wielder to utilize techniques of both spears in harmony.

whaling_knife.name: Whaling Knife
whaling_knife.desc: The knife of one of the infamous Knives of Eylis, mercenaries and assassins feared and reviled the world over. Carries a unique critical that has a higher chance to do Critical Damage if you wield 'Knife's Journey'.

winter_rifle.name: Winter Rifle
winter_rifle.desc: One of Rosen's lesser-known prototypes, this rifle uses a manner of mysticism unbecoming of the mechanical genius.

withered_deaths_knell.name: Withered Death's Knell
withered_deaths_knell.desc: A reminder that all songs must be consumed.

withered_gale_pale.name: Withered Gale Pale
withered_gale_pale.desc: A legacy tarnished by your hand, but granted fell strength by Leiuna's profane forge. The wind is known to hold voices captive and, held in your hands, you hear an earnest plea; Bury the forge. Kill its memory. Greed is their dagger.

withered_phantomcleave.name: Withered Phantomcleave
withered_phantomcleave.desc: The forge's workings singe a memory into the alloyed vessel, thinning its form into translucence. The cast of a vigil swordsman reflects from its depths, appearing sullen and drained of all vitality.

worldpainter_brush.name: Worldpainter Brush
worldpainter_brush.desc: A mighty brush, bestowed upon Lumen's most gifted artisans.

worldpiercer_gauntlets.name: Worldpiercer Gauntlets
worldpiercer_gauntlets.desc: Brutal gauntlets wielded by those who break through defenses with unforgiving force.

worshipper_longsword.name: Worshipper Longsword
worshipper_longsword.desc: A blade forged by a notable Flamepriest, and dedicated to the forge it was reared from.

worshippers_shield.name: Worshipper's Shield
worshippers_shield.desc: A shield bearing an emblem of a Flamepriest. The worship of Fire is seen as embracing a dichotomy between the Fire and the Sea, and places the Deep as the ultimate enemy, with the Fire representing mankind.

wraithclaw.name: Wraithclaw
wraithclaw.desc: Bound and woven with hair and sinew, a spectral claw forms from the grudges of those sacrificed to create it. Across Lumen, many believe that the voices of the dead are carried by the wind. So too do many believe that the winds of Lumen originate from the swirling vortex of the Moonseye above. Perhaps these voices are those of the Old World, mourning and howling at what has become of Lumen.

wretched_mawblades.name: Wretched Mawblades
wretched_mawblades.desc: Born from a twisted experiment by the enigmatic lightborn smiths of the floating keep, the Mawblades are a testament to unspeakable treachery. Stripped of mortality and violently reshaped, the tortured souls within these blades endure a fate crueler than death, their teeth aching eternally as they are bound to the insatiable hunger for destruction.

wyrmtooth.name: Wyrmtooth
wyrmtooth.desc: An exceptionally long and unwieldy blade of Etrean make. Useless in the hands of a novice, but lethal in the hands of one who can adapt to its unnatural centre of gravity.

ysleys_pyre_keeper.name: Ysley's Pyre Keeper
ysleys_pyre_keeper.desc: A pair of blades once used by a High Curator of the Hive. Said to be crafted by the 2nd Greatlord as a Gift to the 3rd, though due to their pacifism it collected dust until acquired by Ysley. Burns brightly with the Fire of Life. Attacks with its dagger offhand scale with LHT.

zweihander.name: Zweihander
zweihander.desc: Popularised by the Canor Borderwatchers, the Zweihander is as fearsome as it is heavy.
